es.title: Noticias Diarias: Copyright e Inteligencia Artificial
es.subtitle: Lo último en leyes de derechos de autor para músicos, compositores y productores.

sections.title: La Corte Suprema de los Estados Unidos se pronuncia sobre los derechos de autor de las obras generadas por IA
sections.content: En un fallo reciente, la Corte Suprema de los Estados Unidos ha establecido que las obras generadas por inteligencia artificial no son elegibles para la protección de derechos de autor. Esta decisión ha generado un gran debate en la industria musical, ya que muchos artistas y productores han comenzado a utilizar la IA para crear música.

sections.title: La empresa de música Amper Music lanza una plataforma de licencias para obras generadas por IA
sections.content: La empresa de música Amper Music ha lanzado una plataforma de licencias para obras generadas por inteligencia artificial. Esta plataforma permite a los artistas y productores obtener licencias para utilizar obras generadas por IA en sus propias creaciones, lo que podría ser un paso importante hacia la clarificación de los derechos de autor en este ámbito.

sections.title: Guía del día: Cómo registrar tus obras generadas por IA
sections.content: Aunque la Corte Suprema de los Estados Unidos ha establecido que las obras generadas por IA no son elegibles para la protección de derechos de autor, es importante que los artistas y productores tomen medidas para proteger sus creaciones. Una forma de hacerlo es registrar sus obras generadas por IA como 'obras derivadas', lo que puede proporcionar alguna protección legal.

en.title: Daily News: Copyright & Artificial Intelligence
en.subtitle: The latest in copyright law for musicians, composers, and producers.

sections.title: US Supreme Court Rules on AI-Generated Works Copyright
sections.content: In a recent ruling, the US Supreme Court has determined that AI-generated works are not eligible for copyright protection. This decision has sparked a significant debate in the music industry, as many artists and producers have begun using AI to create music. Companies like Spotify and Apple Music are closely watching the developments, as they have already started to feature AI-generated content on their platforms.

sections.title: Amper Music Launches Licensing Platform for AI-Generated Works
sections.content: Music company Amper Music has launched a licensing platform for AI-generated works. This platform allows artists and producers to obtain licenses to use AI-generated works in their own creations, which could be an important step towards clarifying copyright in this area. The platform has already gained attention from major music labels, such as Universal Music Group and Sony Music Entertainment.

sections.title: Today's Guide: Registering Your AI-Generated Works
sections.content: Although the US Supreme Court has ruled that AI-generated works are not eligible for copyright protection, it is essential for artists and producers to take steps to protect their creations. One way to do this is to register their AI-generated works as 'derivative works', which can provide some legal protection. The US Copyright Office has issued guidelines on how to register such works, and it is crucial to follow these guidelines to ensure maximum protection.
